title: Julie's Soup Tips
instructions: Pick a fat (olive oil, butter or oil) and place 2-3 tablespoons of fat in soup pot.
Add chopped onion, celery, carrots, and root vegetables and sauté until soft.
Add chopped or smashed garlic and cook, stirring, for about 30 seconds.
If thickening with flour, add now and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring.
Add broth or liquid and heat while stirring occasionally.
Add meat (optional) or any additional items such as canned beans and heat.
Adjust seasonings, add salt and pepper to taste.
notes: You can make soup out of anything!
Be adventurous – try new flavor combinations.

Three types of soups: Broth Soups (chicken, beef or vegetable broth, tomato or vegetable juice), Thick Soups (thickened with roux, cornstarch, pasta, rice, or potatoes), and Pureed Soups (cooked vegetables pureed with broth).

Roux – equal parts butter or oil and flour. Cook vegetables in fat, then sprinkle flour over them OR melt butter/heat oil and add flour. Whisk until cooked, about 3 minutes, before adding broth. For gluten-free, use rice flour.
Cornstarch method – Mix 1-3 tablespoons cornstarch with just enough cold water to form a thick paste. Pour into hot soup and stir. Soup will thicken as it heats. Common in oriental soups.
Pureed Soups – Cook vegetables, add broth, and puree with immersion blender, blender, or food processor.

Great soup seasonings: Nutmeg (flavor enhancer in cream soups), Cumin (depth and heartiness, especially Mexican and Spanish), Cavendar's Greek seasoning (all-purpose enhancer for Greek and cream soups), Cayenne Pepper (subtle heat and flavor enhancement), Garlic Salt (all-purpose but reduce other salt), Garlic Powder (quick garlic addition), Onion Powder (additional flavor), Marjoram/thyme/rosemary (great in beef soups), Bay leaves (earthy flavor for broths and stews), Lemon/lime juice (great in Mexican soups).
